originalTitle: Eight students arrested in Kenya after suspected deadly school arson attack
title: Important News from Kenya: Staying Safe at School
content:
Hello, everyone! It's your friendly news reporter here, bringing you important news from our big continent, Africa. Today, we have some very sad news from a country called Kenya. Kenya is a beautiful country with amazing animals like lions and elephants, and kind people, just like many places in Africa.

A Very Sad Day in Kenya

Something very sad happened at a school in Kenya. A big fire started in a special building where children sleep. This building is called a dormitory. A dormitory is like a very big bedroom where many students live together when they are at a boarding school. Imagine sleeping with all your friends in one big room! That is what a dormitory is for. Sadly, when the fire happened, many children were asleep inside this dormitory. Sixteen pupils, which means sixteen young students, were hurt very badly because of the fire. This made many people very, very sad. It is a very difficult thing to hear about. Our hearts go out to them. Did you know? Many schools in Africa have dormitories because some children live far from their homes. They stay at school all week to learn and only go home on weekends or holidays.

What is a Fire?

A fire is when something burns very fast and makes heat, bright light, and smoke. Sometimes fire can be helpful, like when it warms our homes or cooks our food. We use fire carefully for many good things. But fire can also be very, very dangerous, especially when it is not controlled. When a fire gets too big, it can destroy things like buildings and trees, and it can hurt people. That is why we must always be very careful around fire. The fire at the school in Kenya was a very big and dangerous fire. It burned a whole dormitory. This means the children's sleeping place was destroyed. Imagine your own bedroom suddenly catching fire. That would be very scary and sad! Did you know? Fire needs three things to burn: heat, fuel (like wood or paper), and oxygen (the air we breathe). If you take one away, the fire can stop! That is why firefighters use water to cool the fire and take away the heat.

Who Started the Fire?

Now, this is a very serious part of the story. The police and other grown-ups are looking very carefully into how the fire started. They think someone might have started the fire on purpose. When someone starts a fire on purpose, it is called "arson." Starting a fire on purpose is a very dangerous and very bad thing to do. It can hurt many innocent people and destroy important places like schools, which are meant to be safe. We must always be responsible and think about others. It is important to always be careful with fire. We should never play with matches, lighters, or anything that can make fire. Grown-ups are the only ones who should handle fire, and they do it very carefully and safely. Did you know? If you ever see a fire that is not supposed to be there, or if a grown-up is not around to control it, you should run and tell a trusted adult right away! Don't try to touch it yourself!

What Happens Next?

Because the grown-ups think someone started the fire on purpose, the police need to find out who did it and why. The police are like special helpers who work to keep everyone safe and make sure rules are followed. They are very important problem-solvers in our communities. The news says that eight students have been "arrested." This does not mean they are bad people right away. It means the police have asked them to come and talk to them. The police want to ask questions to understand exactly what happened. They want to find out the truth. The police will talk to these students and others to understand everything. They want to know who might have started the fire and why. It's a bit like when your teacher asks you many questions if something went wrong in the classroom – they want to understand the full story so they can help and make things right. Did you know? Police officers work very hard, day and night, to protect everyone in our communities. They help solve problems, keep the peace, and make sure everyone feels safe.

Keeping Safe at School

This sad event reminds us all how important it is to be safe, especially at school. Our schools are special places where we go to learn, play, and grow. They should always be safe and happy places for us. Schools have very important rules to keep us safe. It's super important to listen to your teachers and other grown-ups. They teach us things like what to do if there is a fire. This is called a fire drill. During a fire drill, everyone learns to walk out of the building calmly and quickly, to a safe spot outside. Did you know? Most schools have fire alarms that make a very loud sound if there is a fire. This sound is a special warning that tells everyone to leave the building safely and quickly. It is also very important to always be kind to each other at school. We should treat our friends and classmates with respect and always try to help them. If you ever see someone doing something dangerous, like playing with fire, you should always tell a grown-up right away. This can help keep everyone safe and prevent accidents.

Sending Love to Kenya

When sad things happen, it's important for us to think about the people who are hurting. We send our thoughts and prayers to all the students, teachers, and families in Kenya who are affected by this terrible fire. We hope they can feel better and find comfort soon. It reminds us that we are all part of one big family in Africa. When one part of our family hurts, we all feel it. We hope that the school can be made safe again very soon, and that all the children can get back to learning and playing in a safe environment. Did you know? Being kind and helping others when they are sad can make a big difference. Even a small kind word or a hug can help someone feel much better when they are going through a tough time. It shows we care. We must all remember to follow safety rules and look out for each other. Let's make sure our schools are always safe places for learning and growing. Be safe, be kind, and keep learning!
summary: A very sad fire at a school in Kenya hurt many children, and the police are talking to some students to find out how it started and keep everyone safe.
whyMatters: This news is important because it reminds us how serious fires can be and why we must always follow safety rules at school to keep ourselves and our friends safe.

originalTitle: Kenya court halts opening of US Ebola quarantine facility in the country
title: Kenya court halts opening of US Ebola quarantine facility in the country
content: A 50-bed isolation unit in Kenya for US citizens was due to open on Friday, a US official said.

originalTitle: Abula - Nigeria's indigenous game with lofty ambitions
title: Abula - Nigeria's indigenous game with lofty ambitions
content: Abula, a Nigerian sport inspired by the Yoruba concept of a balanced meal, aims to grow from a local pastime into an internationally recognised game.

originalTitle: Netanyahu says Israeli forces have crossed Lebanon’s Litani River
title: News from Far Away: A River Story
content:
Hello, wonderful friends! It’s your kid-friendly news reporter here, all the way from sunny Africa! I hope you are having a fantastic day. Today, we have some news to share from a place far away. It’s important to learn about what is happening in our big, wide world. Are you ready to hear a simple story?

A Leader Speaks Out

There is a country called Israel. It is very far from us, across the big ocean and some land. Israel has a leader. This leader is like the captain of a team. He helps make important choices for his country. His name is Mr. Benjamin Netanyahu. He is called the Prime Minister of Israel. The Prime Minister told everyone something interesting. He shared some news about what his country’s special helpers were doing. He said that some people from Israel had moved into another country's land. They went into a place called Lebanon.

Meet Lebanon, a Neighbor

Lebanon is another country. It is right next to Israel. They are like neighbors in a big neighborhood. Each country has its own space. It has its own land, its own homes, and its own special places. Did you know? Our world has many, many countries! They all have different names and different people who live there. Learning about them is fun! When we look at a map, we can see all these different countries. They are often shown in different colors, like a big puzzle.

What is a River?

Now, let's talk about a special part of this news. It involves something called a river. Do you know what a river is? It's like a long, winding path of water. Rivers flow through land, sometimes for a very, very long way. Rivers give us water to drink. They help plants grow. Many animals live near rivers too! This river has a special name. It is called the Litani River. Can you say that? Litani River! This river is in the country of Lebanon. It flows through a part of Lebanon that is in the south. Think of a map of Lebanon. The 'south' part is usually at the bottom of the map. That's where the Litani River flows. Did you know? Rivers are super important! They have been helping people and animals for thousands of years. Some big cities are built right next to rivers!

Special Helpers Called 'Forces'

The Prime Minister, Mr. Netanyahu, talked about 'Israeli forces'. What does that mean? 'Forces' is a word for a country's special team of helpers. These people wear uniforms. Their job is to keep their country safe. They are like the protectors of their homes and their people. They work very hard to make sure everyone in their country is safe and sound. They have special training for their important job. So, when we say 'Israeli forces', we mean these special protectors from Israel. Did you know? Many countries have these special teams! They are there to help if there is trouble and to keep the peace.

Crossing the River: What Happened?

The news is that these Israeli forces 'crossed' the Litani River. What does 'crossed' mean? It means they went from one side of the river to the other side. Imagine you are at one side of a small stream. You jump or step over to the other side. That's crossing! But this was not just crossing any river. This was a river inside *another* country, Lebanon. So, the Prime Minister said that Israel's special team went into Lebanon, across the Litani River. It’s like when you play in your garden. You usually stay in your garden, right? You don't just walk into your neighbor's garden without asking. Countries have 'borders'. These are like invisible lines that show where one country ends and another begins. When a country's team goes over a border or into another country's special space, it's very important news. People all over the world pay attention to things like this. It helps everyone understand what is happening between countries.

Why is this Important?

It is important because countries usually like to stay in their own space. They want to be friends and respect each other's homes. When someone crosses into another country's area, it can make people curious. They want to know why. News reporters, like me, tell people about these things. We want to help everyone understand our big world. Did you know? Most countries try very hard to be good neighbors! They talk to each other and try to solve problems with words, not actions like crossing borders without permission. It’s always a good idea to hope for peace and friendship between all countries. Just like we want to be friends with our neighbors!

Learning About Our World

So, that’s the news from far away. A leader from Israel said that his country’s helpers went across a river in Lebanon. It helps us learn more about countries like Israel and Lebanon, and the important rivers that flow through them. Remember, learning about the world is like a big adventure! Every day, we can discover something new and interesting. Let’s all remember to be kind to our friends and neighbors, both big and small. And let’s hope for a peaceful world for everyone. That’s all for today from your friendly African news reporter! See you next time for more exciting news!
summary: The leader of Israel, called the Prime Minister, said that Israel's special team crossed a river named Litani into another country, Lebanon.
whyMatters: This is important because countries usually stay in their own space, and when one country's team goes into another country, people pay attention to understand why.

originalTitle: Israel, Russia among new additions on UN sexual violence ‘blacklist’
title: Israel, Russia among new additions on UN sexual violence ‘blacklist’
content: New report says nearly 10,000 cases of conflict-related sexual violence were recorded worldwide last year.

content: Kenneth Law admitted charges relating to Canadian victims - but families say he should also be charged in the UK over 79 deaths in Britain.

originalTitle: Nato and EU condemn Russia after drone hits Romanian residential block
title: Nato and EU condemn Russia after drone hits Romanian residential block
content: Romania says the Russian drone was likely hit over Ukraine by its air defences and altered its trajectory.

originalTitle: Barcelona sign Gordon in £69m-plus deal from Newcastle

originalTitle: Mourinho signs three-year deal to return as Real boss
title: Mourinho signs three-year deal to return as Real boss
content: Jose Mourinho signs a three-year deal to become Real Madrid head coach for a second time.

originalTitle: Arsenal's Timber fit to start Champions League final
title: Arsenal's Timber fit to start Champions League final
content: Mikel Arteta says that  Dutch defender Jurrien Timber is fit to start the Champions League final after more than two months out with a groin injury.
